layers.name: Gates
layers.desc: Three kill-gates. Fail one and you stop — before you sink a sprint into the wrong build.

layers.name: Sections
layers.tagline: Write and pressure-test each PRD block
layers.desc: Thirteen skills, one per PRD section. Each forces the hard call and cites its source.

layers.name: Spine
layers.desc: Two skills: one assembles the full PRD, one grades it against the rubric.

differentiatorNote: Four skills carry a Differentiator tag: the eval plan, autonomy/HITL boundaries, the failure premortem, and the learning loop. These are the sections almost no generic PRD writes well; with memory, they are what 'self-learning' actually means.

skills.name: Worth an Agent?
skills.oneLiner: Is an agent the right tool at all?
skills.decides: Ranks options cheapest-first (no-AI script, then workflow + one LLM step, then agent) and demands a measurable problem with a baseline.
skills.do: Require a one-line why-an-agent-not-a-workflow and an ROI.
skills.dont: Approve an agent for a deterministic task, or let "use AI" be the goal.
skills.anchor: Orkes: 40% of agentic projects scrapped by 2027

skills.name: Agent vs Workflow
skills.decides: Places the use case on the spectrum from decider-node to control-flow-owner; picks code-first agent vs durable workflow engine vs hybrid.
skills.do: Reach for a durable engine when it calls external APIs, runs for hours, or must outlive a restart.
skills.dont: Default to "autonomous agent" for hype, or pick a durable engine for a 2-second sync task.
skills.anchor: Principles of Building AI Agents ch4/ch12; Temporal/Conductor

skills.name: Can We Eval It?
skills.decides: Forces a definition of good plus at least one measurable acceptance criterion, and names the eval type.
skills.do: Name the eval type: task-completion, faithfulness, tool-calling, or multi-turn.
skills.dont: Pass on "we'll figure eval out later" or accept "delight users" as success.
skills.anchor: Book: evals are just tests; Mastra scorers

skills.name: Agent Type
skills.oneLiner: Reasoning vs Operational, plus the category.
skills.decides: Picks the cognitive class first, then reuses a standard type from the 15-type taxonomy and the simplest topology.
skills.do: Make the Reasoning-vs-Operational call; it sets eval, cost, and guardrail intensity.
skills.dont: Invent a bespoke type or default to multi-agent.

skills.name: Architecture
skills.oneLiner: The component blueprint, the parts list.
skills.decides: Enumerates every slot: authored context, learned memory, tools, MCP, skills, sub-agents, model + gateway, permissions, surface.
skills.do: Separate authored rules from learned memory and state the deployment surface.
skills.dont: Collapse context, memory, and tools into one box, or leave model-access implicit.

skills.name: Scope & Topology
skills.decides: Writes a capability map and explicit non-goals; starts single-agent and justifies a supervisor only when one agent provably cannot.
skills.do: One job per agent. Justify multi-agent before drawing the org chart.
skills.dont: Design a multi-agent org chart before one agent works, or leave non-goals blank.

skills.name: Tools & Data
skills.decides: Lists the tools first, defines I/O schemas, marks side-effecting tools idempotent, prefers MCP.
skills.do: Put schema constraints in the property description. Give search tools, not a dumped corpus.
skills.dont: Ship a tool without an I/O schema, or trust a third-party MCP server as safe.
skills.anchor: Book: designing your tools is the most important step

skills.name: Memory
skills.decides: Picks a type deliberately: working memory, semantic recall (RAG), observational memory, or none; sets retention and freshness.
skills.do: Append, never wholesale-replace. Keep timestamps and today's date.
skills.dont: Use working memory for long-term recall, or add a vector DB when text memory suffices.

skills.name: Eval Plan
skills.oneLiner: Success metrics and the eval plan.
skills.decides: Defines a golden dataset, a rubric where the LLM emits structured data and code computes the score, thresholds, offline + online evals, and a regression gate.
skills.do: Fail the build on a score drop. Curate + synthesize + mine production logs.
skills.dont: Let an LLM emit a raw 0-1 number, or judge with the agent's own model family.

skills.name: HITL & Autonomy
skills.decides: Places the human gate where the irreversible risk fires, as late as is still safe; distinguishes approval from mid-act suspension.
skills.do: Default to tool-level approval; tighten to agent-level only when the whole op is risky.
skills.dont: Gate everything (alert fatigue), or auto-approve money-movement and destructive actions.
skills.anchor: Mastra HITL trust spectrum; Orkes HUMAN/WAIT

skills.name: Safety & Guardrails
skills.decides: Specs input + output guardrails (injection, PII, jailbreak, toxicity), RBAC, secrets, sandboxing, spend caps, and an audit trail.
skills.do: Fail open by default, fail closed for security. Sandbox agent-generated code.
skills.dont: Rely on the model to self-police, or put credentials in prompts.
skills.anchor: Mastra processors; Book ch9; Orkes RBAC

skills.name: Reliability & Failure
skills.decides: Sets the 4-layer model (task retry, task timeout, workflow timeout, compensation), rate limits, idempotency, a latency budget, and a premortem.
skills.do: Degrade predictably. Write the failure modes before the happy path.
skills.dont: Retry without idempotency, or stretch timeouts instead of going async-poll.

skills.name: Model & Cost
skills.decides: Tiers models (cheap/fast for classification, strong for reasoning/tools), defines a fallback chain, sets a token + latency budget and cost-per-task.
skills.do: Budget cost and latency explicitly. Claude for tool-heavy steps.
skills.dont: Hard-code one provider, or assume a prompt is portable across models.

skills.name: Model Gateway
skills.decides: Scopes virtual keys with hard budgets, defines overrun and unreachable-backend behavior, attributes spend, allows BYOK.
skills.do: Fail closed on budget overrun. Attribute spend per caller.
skills.dont: Ship raw provider keys to the runtime, or assume one provider's wire format.

skills.name: Observability & Ops
skills.decides: Specs tracing (spans + token/cost), metrics (latency, cost, scores, errors), drift detection, a runbook, dev-to-prod promotion, and versioning.
skills.do: Track decision quality, not just infra metrics. Version looping workflows.
skills.dont: Ship without tracing (agents regress while returning 200 OK).

skills.name: Learning Loop
skills.decides: Specifies how the agent improves: production traces, datasets-from-prod, re-eval, regression gate; what is automated vs human-reviewed.
skills.do: Be honest that prompt/tool rewrites are still human-in-the-loop today.
skills.dont: Claim self-learning without this section, or promise fully-automatic retraining.

skills.name: Agent PRD
skills.decides: Intakes the idea, runs the gates, drives the section skills, and assembles the doc with requirement IDs and depth control.
skills.do: Run the three gates first and stop if any kills. Keep an open-questions log.
skills.dont: Generate sections the gates have not cleared, or invent facts to fill a gap.

skills.name: PRD Review
skills.decides: Scores a draft against the agentic rubric; rejects if there is no eval plan, no HITL boundary, or no failure premortem.
skills.do: Check all sections exist and every claim is falsifiable.
skills.dont: Pass a PRD that says reliable or self-learning without the trio.
